channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Who will win the World Cup? BBC pundits make their predictions
item.description: Who will win, who might spring a surprise and how will England and Scotland do? BBC pundits predict what will happen at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: The wild hip-hop parties that started Tuchel's journey to England boss
item.description: Thomas Tuchel is bidding to become the first foreign manager to win the World Cup. BBC Sport charts the start of his journey to becoming England boss.

item.title: Keane and Fernandes clear air after row over 'lie'
item.description: Roy Keane clears the air with Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes after he misquoted the Portuguese - which led to Fernandes accusing him of lying.

item.title: Banned referee Artan arrives home in Somalia
item.description: Omar Artan vows to officiate at the 2030 World Cup instead after arriving home in Somalia in the wake of being banned from entering the United States.

item.title: Why this World Cup will showcase 'different Steve Clarke'
item.description: The Scotland head coach feels like a new man heading into a seismic World Cup opener with Haiti - and he is sensing a fresh wave of optimism.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 3
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: When will an African side win the World Cup?
item.description: Morocco became Africa's first World Cup semi-finalists at Qatar 2022, but how close is a side from the continent to lifting the trophy?

item.title: England miss out on automatic World Cup spot - so what happens now?
item.description: England beat Ukraine 3-0 on Tuesday night but it did not matter what they did as automatic qualification for the 2027 Women's World Cup was out of their hands.

item.title: Real Madrid's £130m Alvarez bid laughed off by Atletico
item.description: Real Madrid make an offer of 150m euros (£130m) for striker Julian Alvarez, but city rivals Atletico Madrid turn it down.

item.title: Real Madrid eye Arsenal's Calafiori - Wednesday's gossip
item.description: Real Madrid scout Arsenal's Riccardo Calafiori, Tottenham consider Juventus defender Gleison Bremer, Manchester United chase Newcastle's Lewis Hall, plus more.

item.title: Scotland 'living dream' thanks to 'humble' superstar Weir
item.description: Caroline Weir insists it is a team effort after her four goals against Israel ensure Scotland top their Women's World Cup qualifying group.

item.title: Play BBC Sport's new World Cup predictor game
item.description: Play BBC Sport's new World Cup predictor game to be in with the chance of winning a signed football.

item.title: How to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
item.description: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup group games.

item.title: Download your World Cup 2026 wallchart
item.description: Get your essential piece of kit now by downloading the Fifa World Cup 2026 wallchart.

item.title: Does referee case show Fifa has lost control of its own World Cup?
item.description: With referee Omar Artan denied entry to the United States and worries over staff and supporters being turned back, what does this tell us about the World Cup?

item.title: Sullivan banned from contact with West Ham women's and youth teams since 2023
item.description: The decision followed a safeguarding investigation opened by the the Football Association.

item.title: Caution over Saka as Tuchel says England must dare to dream
item.description: England head coach Thomas Tuchel says Bukayo Saka's fitness is being managed with a "little bit of care" heading into the World Cup.

item.title: From risk of death to oldest man at World Cup - walking miracle Gordon
item.description: How Craig Gordon's career has been a series of comebacks and the 43-year-old's World Cup inclusion for Scotland seemed an impossible dream only a few months ago.

item.title: World Cup 1982: Stein, Narey, Brazil & being a cartoon character
item.description: Joe Jordan, Gordon Strachan and John Wark recall Scotland's 1982 World Cup campaign.

item.title: 'Rashford's a dead cert' – Rooney, Walcott & Defoe on England's wide options
item.description: Wayne Rooney, Theo Walcott, Jermain Defoe, Kae Kurd and Kelly Somers assess England's wide options for the World Cup.

item.title: Watch: Weir scores four as Scotland beat Israel to win group
item.description: Watch the highlights as Scotland beat Israel 5-1, with Caroline Weir scoring four goals in Hungary to top their Women's World Cup qualifying group ahead of Belgium and win promotion to League A for the next Nations League.

item.title: Highlights: Wales beat Czech Republic 3-1 to top group
item.description: Watch highlights as Wales beat Czech Republic in Cardiff in Women's World Cup qualifying.

item.title: Highlights: Switzerland sweep aside Northern Ireland
item.description: Watch highlights as group winners Switzerland finish their World Cup qualifying campaign with a 2-1 win over Northern Ireland in Lurgan.

item.title: 'You're paid to make that decision' – Eriksson's midfield dilemma
item.description: Former players and coaching staff from England's 2006 'Golden Generation' reflect on how manager Sven-Goran Eriksson struggled to get the best out of Steven Gerrard and Frank Lampard in a star-studded midfield.

item.title: 'I designed football shirts for World cup winners'
item.description: Rob Warner created Italy's royal blue kit in which they lifted the trophy in 2006.

item.title: Ex-England midfielder Lee takes up Hornchurch role
item.description: Hornchurch appoint former England and Newcastle United midfielder Rob Lee as their new director of football.

item.title: Silva agrees to replace Mourinho at Benfica
item.description: Marco Silva agrees to become Benfica's new head coach as the Portuguese club formalises Jose Mourinho's departure for Real Madrid.

item.title: Tottenham defender Davies signs new one-year deal
item.description: Experienced Tottenham Hotspur and Wales defender Ben Davies takes his stay at the north London club into a 13th year.

item.title: What now for West Ham after Sullivan allegations?
item.description: West Ham is a club in turmoil after co-chair David Sullivan stood down ahead of allegations about his private life, which he denies.

item.title: Why super-sized and politicised World Cup comes at a cost
item.description: Whatever one's perspective, it seems certain that away from the on-field spectacle, this super-sized World Cup could be among the most contentious ever.

item.title: Olise scores hat-trick as France beat Northern Ireland
item.description: Bayern Munich's Michael Olise scores a hat-trick in France's 3-1 victory over Northern Ireland in their final World Cup warm-up match in Lille.

item.title: Norway dress up as Vikings for World Cup
item.description: Watch behind-the-scenes footage as the Norway squad dress up as Vikings for a team photo before the World Cup.

item.title: Scotland score four in first half to beat Bolivia
item.description: Watch the goals as Scotland score four in the first half to beat Bolivia in their final World Cup warm-up.

item.title: Highlights: Romania 2-1 Wales
item.description: Watch the best of the action as Romania defeat Wales 2-1 in Bucharest.

item.title: 'The things that forge them are similar' - the origins of Messi & Ronaldo
item.description: Both from humble beginnings, BBC Sport looks at how Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo became two of the greatest footballers of all time.

item.title: Watch: Weir hat-trick as Scotland thump Israel
item.description: Watch as Scotland thump Israel 6-0 in World Cup qualifying Group B4, with captain Caroline Weir scoring a hat-trick.

item.title: Highlights: Wales held to draw in Montenegro
item.description: Watch highlights as Montenegro and Wales draw in Podgorica in Women's World Cup qualifying.

item.title: Watch: When Hagi's Romania thrashed Wales
item.description: Wales travel to Bucharest to face Romania on Saturday for the first time since their World Cup qualifying defeat in 1992.

item.title: Messi or Ronaldo - who is the greatest of all time?
item.description: Some of the greats of the game debate the long-asked question: Lionel Messi or Cristiano Ronaldo?

item.title: 'Mazadona', 'Einstein', 'The Ant' - 20 new players to watch at the 2026 World Cup
item.description: BBC Sport TV and radio commentators pick the players they think could be the breakout stars of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Pain in Grenoble but hope for Irish as play-offs loom
item.description: Republic of Ireland manager Carla Ward reacts with pride to the World Cup qualifying campaign as her side fall just short of an automatic place at next year's tournament.

item.title: 'Gridlock alert days' and a race for tickets as US prepares to host World Cup
item.description: Is the US ready to handle the pressure of co-hosting what many see as the biggest global sporting event?

item.title: Fifa working to resolve revoked Iran tickets
item.description: Fifa has said it is working to "maximise opportunities for Iranian supporters to attend matches" after the country's World Cup group-stage ticket allocation was revoked just days before the tournament.

item.title: Scoring on NI debut unbelievable - Cassap
item.description: Northern Ireland debutant Emily Cassap says her strike just 66 seconds after her introduction was "probably the best goal I have ever scored".

item.title: The flag Iranians are not allowed to wave at the World Cup
item.description: Iran football fans living in Los Angeles explain why they want to wave Iran's pre-1979 Islamic revolution flag and dismiss the one that is on their team's shirt.

item.title: Wolves re-sign striker Jimenez from Fulham
item.description: Wolves re-sign striker Raul Jimenez from Premier League Fulham on a free transfer.

item.title: Two brothers, two nations, one World Cup & how grief bound them
item.description: In July 2022, brothers Harry and John Souttar lost their hero, but as both prepare to represent different nations at the World Cup, his influence lives on.

item.title: Ngumoha to leave England squad having made mark
item.description: Rio Ngumoha will leave the US this week after impressing with England's World Cup squad but will return to the country for Liverpool's pre-season tour next month.

item.title: 'Diogo will be with you' - Jota's widow writes to Robertson
item.description: Diogo Jota's widow urges Scotland captain Andrew Robertson to carry his former Liverpool team-mate "in your heart" when he plays at the World Cup.

item.title: How can athletes play with ICDs, and what happens when one goes off?
item.description: BBC Sport explores how athletes with cardiac health issues are able to continue playing, the risks, and what happens when an ICD goes off.

item.title: From dazzling winger to veteran who barely runs - the evolution of Messi
item.description: Lionel Messi is set for his sixth World Cup with Argentina. Guillem Balague looks at how the Argentine has evolved.

item.title: Born in Leeds, crafted in Norway: How Haaland became nation's superstar
item.description: Erling Haaland has guided Norway to their first World Cup since 1998. So what is it like being a global name from a nation with a population of just 5.5m people?

item.title: Are Portugal better without Ronaldo - and is he undroppable?
item.description: As Cristiano Ronaldo prepares for his sixth World Cup with Portugal, BBC Sport take a closer look at his place in the team.

item.title: The pioneer in an unlikely World Cup team
item.description: When Desmond Armstrong faced the media at the World Cup in 1990, the first question he got was one that would stick in his mind for the wrong reasons.

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What are the deadlines for World Cup squads?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the deadlines by which teams must name their final squads for the 2026 World Cup

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: Which club received most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the teams who have received the most red cards in a Premier League season.

item.title: Who won the Premier League golden boot?
item.description: BBC's Ask Me Anything team explains who has won the Premier League golden boot and other individual awards.

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League assists in a season?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at who has provided the most assists in the history of the Premier League.

item.title: Celtic linked with Tahirovic as Reynolds attracts Rangers - gossip
item.description: Midfielder linked with Celtic as Rangers look at right-back while Dundee United face competition for winger.

item.title: Fresh faces to managerial mayhem - NI's qualifying campaign
item.description: A World Cup group qualifying campaign with many talking points for Northern Ireland comes to an end with the main objective achieved - keeping the World Cup dream alive.

item.title: Wales can reach Women's World Cup - Wilkinson
item.description: Rhian Wilkinson says Wales have the potential to qualify for next year's Women's World Cup after they beat the Czech Republic to claim top spot in Group B1.

item.title: 'Two of my children play for England, I still pinch myself'
item.description: Nigel James says "I still pinch myself" that two of his children represent England's football teams.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.
